display.purpose: Page-level section headings. One per view.

headline.purpose: Widget headings, card titles, modal headings.

title.purpose: Table column headers, sidebar section labels, form group labels.

body.purpose: All prose content, descriptions, task fields. Max 72ch.

label.purpose: Status badge text, role chips, metadata tags. Uppercase only at this size.

mono.purpose: Employee IDs, task reference numbers, schema field names, log entries.

shadows.name: card-rest
shadows.purpose: Cards at rest use border-only separation. No shadow.

shadows.name: card-hover
shadows.purpose: Lift on interactive summary widgets on hover only.

shadows.name: popover
shadows.purpose: Dropdown menus and popovers floating above page content.

shadows.name: modal
shadows.purpose: Modal dialogs. Appears with backdrop overlay.

shadows.name: button-primary
shadows.purpose: Primary button resting shadow. Signal Blue glow, the one deliberate use of a coloured shadow.

motion.name: ease-standard
motion.purpose: Default easing for state transitions (color, background, border).

components.name: Primary Button
components.description: Primary action trigger. Signal Blue. Used for form submission, creating records, confirming actions.
components.css: .ds-btn-primary { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; gap: 8px; background: #2563eb; color: #ffffff; font-family: 'Geist Sans', system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 0.875rem; font-weight: 600; line-height: 1; padding: 12px 20px; border: none; border-radius: 12px; box-shadow: 0 2px 6px rgba(37,99,235,0.25); cursor: pointer; transition: background 150ms cubic-bezier(0.4,0,0.2,1), box-shadow 150ms cubic-bezier(0.4,0,0.2,1), transform 100ms cubic-bezier(0.4,0,0.2,1); } .ds-btn-primary:hover { background: #1d4ed8; box-shadow: 0 4px 12px rgba(37,99,235,0.30); } .ds-btn-primary:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid #2563eb; outline-offset: 2px; } .ds-btn-primary:active { transform: scale(0.97); } .ds-btn-primary:disabled { opacity: 0.6; cursor: not-allowed; transform: none; } @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .ds-btn-primary { transition: none; transform: none; } }

components.name: Ghost Button
components.description: Secondary action. Transparent with border. Used alongside a primary button.
components.css: .ds-btn-ghost { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; gap: 8px; background: transparent; color: #475569; font-family: 'Geist Sans', system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 0.875rem; font-weight: 500; line-height: 1; padding: 12px 20px; border: 1px solid #e2e8f0; border-radius: 12px; cursor: pointer; transition: background 150ms cubic-bezier(0.4,0,0.2,1), color 150ms cubic-bezier(0.4,0,0.2,1); } .ds-btn-ghost:hover { background: #f8fafc; color: #1e293b; } .ds-btn-ghost:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid #2563eb; outline-offset: 2px; } @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .ds-btn-ghost { transition: none; } }

components.name: Status Badge — Caution
components.description: Overdue tasks, escalations, warnings. Amber. The most common non-blue colour in the system.
components.css: .ds-badge { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; font-family: 'Geist Sans', system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 0.75rem; font-weight: 600; letter-spacing: 0.04em; text-transform: uppercase; padding: 2px 10px; border-radius: 9999px; white-space: nowrap; } .ds-badge-caution { background: #fffbeb; color: #d97706; }

components.name: Status Badge — Finding
components.description: Open findings, errors, validation failures. Red. Highest urgency.

components.name: Status Badge — Clear
components.description: Closed findings, completed tasks, cleared work packages. Emerald. Signals resolution.

components.name: Status Badge — Active
components.description: In-progress tasks, active states. Signal Blue surface.

components.name: Text Input
components.description: Standard text field. Used on forms for task data, filters, search.
components.css: .ds-field { display: flex; flex-direction: column; gap: 4px; } .ds-field-label { font-family: 'Geist Sans', system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 0.875rem; font-weight: 600; color: #1e293b; } .ds-input { font-family: 'Geist Sans', system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 0.875rem; font-weight: 400; color: #1e293b; background: #ffffff; border: 1px solid #e2e8f0; border-radius: 12px; padding: 12px 16px; width: 100%; outline: none; transition: box-shadow 120ms cubic-bezier(0.4,0,0.2,1), border-color 120ms cubic-bezier(0.4,0,0.2,1); } .ds-input::placeholder { color: #475569; } .ds-input:focus { border-color: transparent; box-shadow: 0 0 0 2px #2563eb; } @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .ds-input { transition: none; } }

components.name: Sidebar Navigation Item
components.description: Left sidebar navigation. Default, hover, and active states. Active state = Signal Blue surface.
components.html: DashboardTasks3Work Packages
components.css: .ds-sidebar-nav { display: flex; flex-direction: column; gap: 2px; padding: 16px; } .ds-nav-item { display: flex; align-items: center; gap: 12px; padding: 10px 12px; border-radius: 12px; font-family: 'Geist Sans', system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 0.875rem; font-weight: 400; color: #475569; text-decoration: none; transition: background 150ms cubic-bezier(0.4,0,0.2,1), color 150ms cubic-bezier(0.4,0,0.2,1); } .ds-nav-item:hover { background: #f8fafc; color: #1e293b; } .ds-nav-item--active { background: #eff6ff; color: #2563eb; font-weight: 500; } .ds-nav-item--active svg { color: #2563eb; } .ds-nav-badge { margin-left: auto; display: inline-flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; min-width: 20px; height: 20px; padding: 0 6px; border-radius: 9999px; background: #fffbeb; color: #d97706; font-size: 0.75rem; font-weight: 700; } @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .ds-nav-item { transition: none; } }

components.name: Metric Card
components.description: Dashboard summary widget. White card, border, metric value, subtitle, icon with tinted background.
components.html:
12
Pending Tasks
Tasks assigned to you
components.css: .ds-metric-card { display: flex; align-items: flex-start; gap: 16px; background: #ffffff; border: 1px solid #e2e8f0; border-radius: 12px; padding: 24px; } .ds-metric-icon { display: flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; width: 48px; height: 48px; border-radius: 12px; flex-shrink: 0; } .ds-metric-icon--caution { background: #fffbeb; color: #d97706; } .ds-metric-icon--finding { background: #fef2f2; color: #dc2626; } .ds-metric-icon--blue { background: #eff6ff; color: #2563eb; } .ds-metric-content { display: flex; flex-direction: column; gap: 2px; } .ds-metric-value { font-family: 'Geist Sans', system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 2rem; font-weight: 700; line-height: 1; color: #1e293b; letter-spacing: -0.02em; } .ds-metric-title { font-family: 'Geist Sans', system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 0.9375rem; font-weight: 600; color: #1e293b; margin-top: 4px; } .ds-metric-sub { font-family: 'Geist Sans', system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 0.75rem; font-weight: 400; color: #94a3b8; }

narrative.overview:
SQD-APP is designed like a well-structured technical manual: every element earns its place through function, hierarchy is derived from information importance, and no decoration is applied without a structural reason. The system communicates authority through precision — tight typographic rhythm, a disciplined neutral palette, and a status-color vocabulary that fires only when something genuinely demands attention.

This is a tool used by maintenance technicians and quality officers under operational conditions. It must be readable at a glance, scannable under time pressure, and absolutely unambiguous about status. The interface does not attempt to charm or engage — it informs and enables. Restraint is the aesthetic: the absence of noise is itself the signal that the system is under control.

The palette is slate-forward with a single Signal Blue accent that marks interactive elements and active states. Status colours — amber for caution, red for findings and overdue, emerald for cleared/complete — appear only in badges and alert surfaces, never as decorative elements.
keyCharacteristics: Dense-but-legible: tight rows, clear typographic hierarchy, breathing room earned through structure not padding
Status-first: finding counts, overdue indicators, and escalation badges are impossible to miss
Role-adaptive: the interface surfaces what each role needs
Flat-by-default: depth is structural not decorative
Monochromatic discipline: Signal Blue does exactly one job; status colours do theirs; the rest is slate

rules.name: The One Signal Rule
rules.body: Signal Blue is the only colour used for interactive affordances. No secondary accent, no gradient treatment. If the user needs to act on something, it is blue. If it is not blue, it is for reading.

rules.name: The Status Firewall Rule
rules.body: Amber, Red, and Emerald are status colours, not brand colours. They appear only on status chips, alert surfaces, badge counts, and system-reported conditions. Prohibited on buttons, illustrations, or any decorative element.

rules.name: The Weight-Not-Size Rule
rules.body: Hierarchy is expressed through weight contrast before size contrast. Reserve size increases for genuine level jumps.

rules.name: The Uppercase Ceiling Rule
rules.body: Uppercase with tracked letterspacing is limited to Labels (badges, metadata chips). Prohibited on headings, body copy, buttons, or any element larger than 12px.

rules.name: The Flat-By-Default Rule
rules.body: Surfaces are flat at rest. A shadow that appears without a user action (hover, focus, modal open) is a decoration masquerading as structure — forbidden.

rules.name: The One-Badge Rule
rules.body: No element carries more than one status badge. If two statuses apply, surface the higher-urgency one. The interface resolves conflicts; it does not pile them on the user.

dos: Do use Signal Blue exclusively for interactive affordances (buttons, links, active nav, focus rings).
Do use status colours (Amber, Red, Emerald) only on live system-reported conditions: badge counts, status chips, alert surfaces.
Do keep body text at Ink Primary (#1e293b) on Surface Card (#ffffff) — confirmed 4.5:1+ contrast.
Do use Geist Mono for employee IDs, task reference numbers, schema field names, and system-generated identifiers.
Do express hierarchy through font weight contrast before font size contrast.
Do keep cards flat at rest. Border separation is structural; shadow at rest is decoration.
Do verify every new status badge achieves 4.5:1 contrast between text and its surface background.
Do use text-wrap: balance on page-level headings to prevent orphan lines.
donts: Don't use gradient text (background-clip: text with a gradient). Prohibited on all elements.
Don't make the interface look like a consumer SaaS product: no playful blob backgrounds, no pastel accents, no whitespace-as-personality.
Don't tip into military-ops UI theatre: no dark-mode-by-default, no amber glows, no radar-screen aesthetics.
Don't use border-left or border-right wider than 1px as a coloured stripe accent on cards, list items, or alerts.
Don't apply hover shadows to non-interactive data cards.
Don't use uppercase tracked letters above 12px or on more than one element per component.
Don't introduce a second accent colour without a specific, documented status role.
Don't nest cards. Use internal dividers and section headings within a single card instead.
Don't rely on colour alone to communicate status. Every status badge carries a text label alongside its colour.
Don't use Ink Muted (#94a3b8) for placeholder text — it's ~2.4:1 on white, failing WCAG 1.4.3 AA. Use Ink Secondary (#475569) instead.
